summary: Admin Dashboard end-to-end validation (backend + Next.js web admin at :3009). Backend: 19/19 pytest tests passed (100%) covering admin auth gate, /api/admin/overview shape (KPIs, 14-pt series, breakdowns, recent_orders<=8), full CRUD-like flows on drivers/shippers/orders/vehicles, suspend/activate cycle with persistence verified via GET, cancel-on-already-delivered returns 400, reassign unknown driver returns 404, and suspension enforcement on driver POST /toggle-online (403) and shipper POST /shipments (403). Demo driver and shipper were re-activated and login still works (200/200). Frontend: web admin login with admin@nadaruns.com/admin123 succeeds, Overview renders KPIs + deliveries/revenue charts + status donut + vehicle bars, all sidebar tabs (Drivers, Shippers, Orders, Vehicles, KYC) navigate and load real seeded data, driver row opens detail drawer with edit form + vehicles + Save changes + Suspend buttons, order row opens drawer with full order details (Pickup/Dropoff/Vehicle/Weight/Distance/Price/Tip/Earnings/Parties/Timeline), and KYC tab shows pending+reviewed sections. Cancel/Reassign buttons render only for active orders (delivered/cancelled hide them) which is correct.

issues: No data-testid / testID attributes on interactive elements (sidebar buttons, list rows, drawer Save/Suspend/Cancel/Reassign, login inputs). Tests had to fall back to role/text selectors. Adding testIDs would make UI regression tests far more reliable.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Add data-testid attributes to admin login inputs, sidebar nav buttons, list-row click targets, and drawer action buttons (Save changes / Suspend / Activate / Cancel order / Reassign) to make future automated tests more robust.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 4976 lines long — well over the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting admin endpoints (overview + manage/* router) into a dedicated module (e.g. backend/routers/admin.py) in a future refactor.
Admin password is read from env with a hardcoded fallback of 'admin123' (matches the requested env-based design).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 6 tabs load, login + drawer + edit form working)

seed_data_creation: Re-ran POST /api/seed-demo at test session start (idempotent; returned 0 newly created on this run). Tests created two TEST_-prefixed shipments via demo shipper; one was cancelled by the admin cancel-pending test (verified status='cancelled' via GET), the other was the suspension-enforcement attempt which was correctly blocked (403, no row created). Demo driver and demo shipper were SUSPENDED during enforcement tests and then RE-ACTIVATED in `finally` blocks; final login probes returned 200/200 for both demo accounts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All admin endpoints live in /app/backend/server.py around lines 3805-4220. Web admin is at /app/web (Next.js, dev on :3009) and reads NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL from /app/web/.env.local. Pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_admin_dashboard.py use a `_valid_shipment_payload` helper that matches the real ShipmentCreateRequest schema (pickup_/dropoff_ + cargo_weight_kg + cargo_description + urgency + contact name/phone) — reuse it. The web admin has no testIDs; selectors use role/text.
